text.title: QA Rollback Trigger Canon Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-rollback-trigger-canon methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Triggers are declared and reviewed BEFORE the release; mid-incident threshold debates are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mid-incident debates waste rollback budget; pre-declared triggers cut decision latency.

rule.statement: Each trigger names a monitored signal (metric path + label selector). Vague 'feels slow' triggers are rejected.
Each trigger has a numeric threshold AND a continuous observation window (≥2 min, ≤15 min) to avoid single-spike rollback.
rule.rationale: Single-spike rollbacks thrash deploys; sustained-window triggers are stable.

rule.statement: Each trigger names a single on-call role (handle/PagerDuty schedule). 'Team' is not an owner.
rule.rationale: Without a name the ack is delayed.

rule.statement: Each trigger is either auto-rollback or one-click rollback by named owner. No 'open ticket and wait' triggers.
Every trigger firing produces a recorded decision (rolled back yes/no, reason, owner). Even no-action firings are logged.
rule.rationale: Logged firings let us tune thresholds; unlogged firings produce alert fatigue.

rule.statement: If no rollback mechanism exists OR no monitoring OR release is compliance-locked, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Triggers are wasted without the substrate to act on them.
